title: Seed the excluded-resources register (dbGaP/EGA/biobanks, pointer-only)
context: Controlled-access and identifiable resources must never appear as catalog records; however, users should not be misled into thinking these resources are unavailable. The project's design calls for an excluded-resources register: a short, explicit list of controlled/identifiable resources recorded only as 'excluded — controlled, contact custodian' pointers, with the exact application path so users know how to request access legitimately. The register must be seeded before the M0 pilot so the gate (gate-003) has a documented reference for the excluded set. The register must include at minimum: dbGaP controlled studies, EGA studies, TCGA/ICGC controlled tiers (including germline + raw sequence/BAMs), GTEx, TOPMed, and individual-level biobanks (UK Biobank, Genomics England, All of Us, MVP). Depends on gate-003 for the policy framing.
objective: Create and commit the excluded-resources register seeding the known set of controlled/identifiable resources (dbGaP, EGA, TCGA/ICGC controlled tiers, GTEx, TOPMed, UK Biobank, Genomics England, All of Us, MVP) as pointer-only entries with application paths, so users are warned rather than misled and the gate has a reference for the excluded set.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed excluded-resources register (e.g. EXCLUDED.md or excluded-resources.json) lists every initially-known controlled/identifiable resource as a pointer entry: resource name, custodian, why excluded (controlled tier / identifiable / DUA-required), and the application/access path for legitimate use.

Register includes at minimum: dbGaP (NCBI controlled studies), EGA (EMBL-EBI controlled), TCGA controlled tier (dbGaP DAR; germline + raw BAMs explicitly noted), ICGC controlled tier, GTEx, TOPMed, UK Biobank, Genomics England, All of Us, MVP.
No record-level metadata is extracted for any excluded resource; the register entry is a pointer only (no cohort counts, no field values, no license breakdown for the controlled resource itself).
Each pointer entry states the correct application path (e.g. dbGaP authorized access, EGA access committee) so users are not simply told 'excluded' with no path forward.

Register design makes clear that a future resource may move from excluded to indexed only if the gate confirms an open-access tier exists (e.g. a mixed-tier resource like TCGA has its open tier indexed separately).
output: A committed excluded-resources register (document format) listing controlled/identifiable resources as pointer-only entries with application paths, seeding the known excluded set for the gate and for user guidance.
